x-control-room-idle-title: Your room is ready to play
x-control-room-idle-subtitle: Server, paths, player, TV, and AV in one view.
x-migration-description: A compatible configuration was found. Import it or start fresh.
x-legacy-import-description: You can load your previous installation's config.json instead of setting everything up again.
x-legacy-import-warning: After importing, review every section: if Emby isn't reachable right now or some data isn't recognized, you'll need to fill it in by hand.
x-legacy-import-invalid-file: That file doesn't look like a valid config.json.
x-media-server-title: Your library, ready for the room
x-media-server-subtitle: Connect your media server so HCC can detect what you choose and prepare playback on the OPPO.
x-media-server-tooltip-provider: Choose which media server Home Cinema Control connects to. Changing it clears the saved token and monitored device until you authorize again.
x-media-server-password-hint: Your password is never stored — it's only used once to obtain a media server access token.
x-media-server-password-placeholder: Used once to obtain a token
x-media-server-test-apply-hint: If the test succeeds and HCC is not connected to the media server yet, this connection is applied to the service.
x-media-server-device-hint: When you start playback from the media server app on this device, Home Cinema Control stops it and triggers the full cinema sequence: media player, TV and AV receiver.
x-media-server-no-devices-copy: {server} did not return available clients. Check that the server is reachable, then refresh again.
x-media-server-readiness-no-device: {server} did not return the monitored device
x-media-server-no-library-paths-copy: {type} did not return library paths. Configure at least one library with real folders in the media server before mapping paths.
x-media-server-switch-needs-login: {server} is prepared. Log in to activate it.
x-media-server-switch-confirm: Playback is currently active on {server}. Switching means it stops being controlled from that app, though Home Cinema Control will still finish it out normally. Continue?
x-media-server-session-expired-copy: The stored token for {server} is no longer valid. Sign in again to reconnect.
x-media-server-connect-error-unreachable: {server} didn't respond. Check that it's running and reachable on your network, then try again.
x-media-server-connect-error-generic: Couldn't complete the connection to {server}. Check the server address and try again.
x-media-server-tooltip-connection: Defines the media server Home Cinema Control watches for playback sessions.
x-media-server-tooltip-url: Use the media server's base URL, including protocol and port. Example: http://192.168.1.10:8096.
x-media-server-tooltip-auth: The password is only used to request a media server token. The token is stored as a secret and is not shown afterwards.
x-media-server-tooltip-device: Choose the media server client where you start playback. HCC only intercepts sessions from that device.
x-media-player-subtitle: Set its network and playback behavior so HCC can mount paths and start playback without manual steps.
x-media-player-bluray-mode: Blu-ray disc mode (double EJECT on power on)
x-media-player-defaults-restored: Advanced values restored — remember to save.
x-media-player-tooltip-connection: You only need the OPPO IP to start. Timeouts stay under advanced configuration because they depend on hardware and network behavior.
x-media-player-tooltip-ip: Local network IP address of the OPPO player or Chinoppo clone.
x-media-player-tooltip-options: Controls how the OPPO participates in the playback and shutdown sequence.
x-media-player-tooltip-always-on: Enable this if the OPPO is usually already powered on. If disabled, HCC will try to power it off after playback.
x-media-player-tooltip-timeout-connectio: Maximum time to wait for the OPPO control API to respond.
x-media-player-tooltip-timeout-playback-: Maximum time to confirm that the OPPO has started playing the file.
x-media-player-tooltip-timeout-nfs-mount: Maximum time to mount the network folder on the OPPO, for both NFS and SMB/CIFS — it's the same timeout for both protocols. Also used by path tests.
x-media-player-tooltip-autoscript: Indicates that the OPPO manages mounts through autoscript. Leave it off if you do not use that legacy flow.
x-media-server-tooltip-reload-devices: Reloads the list of clients detected by the media server.
x-media-server-tooltip-library-paths: These are the physical folders reported by the media server. HCC uses them later to create the mapping to the OPPO-visible path.
x-paths-tooltip-refresh-paths: Refreshes saved paths and their status from the current configuration.
x-paths-tooltip-discover: Finds library paths from the media server to help you create mappings.
x-paths-tooltip-smb-clear: Deletes the saved SMB/CIFS credentials from secrets.
x-paths-tooltip-edit-server-path: Lets you correct the physical path returned by Emby, Jellyfin, or Plex. Use it when the detected library path does not match the real path, or when you need a manual mapping.
x-paths-summary: {detected} libraries detected · {verified} verified paths · {active} intercepted · {pending} pending
x-paths-select-library: Select a library to connect its path with the OPPO.
x-paths-resolution-copy: Connect this path detected by {type} with the path the player sees.
x-paths-oppo-browser-hint: Browse the real path returned by the OPPO for the selected protocol. Do not reuse paths from another protocol.
x-paths-player-suggestion-title: Suggestion based on a verified path
x-paths-no-detected-copy: {type} did not return physical folders to configure. You can create a manual mapping if you know the real path.
x-paths-advanced-copy: Manually correct the physical path returned by the media server. The OPPO path still has to be tested before it is saved as verified.
x-paths-library-filter-copy: This decides where HCC acts when playback starts. It does not delete or invalidate already configured paths.
x-paths-status-legend-verified: The path has been tested and HCC can use it if the library is intercepted.
x-paths-status-legend-not-intercepted: The path is ready, but HCC will not act until this library is enabled or all libraries are used.
x-paths-status-legend-pending: A path is saved, but it has not been tested successfully yet.
x-paths-status-legend-stale: The path was verified before, but it changed and needs another test.
x-paths-status-legend-unconfigured: No OPPO path is configured for this library.
x-paths-status-legend-error: The last test failed. Check protocol, path, and credentials.
x-paths-smb-active-copy: The OPPO will browse and test paths using SMB/CIFS.
x-paths-generic-test-suggestion: Check the OPPO-visible path, network mode, and SMB credentials if you use SMB/CIFS.
x-paths-subtitle: Connect what the server sees with what the OPPO can mount, then verify each path before using it.
x-paths-no-paths: No paths yet — add one below.
x-paths-nav-error: OPPO is not reachable. Make sure the player is on.
x-paths-nav-mount-error: Could not mount the folder. Try again.
x-paths-path-stale: Path changed — test again to verify.
x-paths-test-hint: Tests that the OPPO can mount the network share. Does not validate the media-server path.
x-paths-field-source-hint: Example: /volume1/media/movies — accept the suggestion or copy it from your library settings ({type}).
x-paths-field-player-hint: Example: //192.168.1.100/movies — enter the NFS or SMB path the OPPO can mount first.
x-paths-tip: Start with the OPPO-visible network path. If you already have a verified mapping, Home Cinema Control can infer the matching media server path and you can adjust it before saving.
x-paths-smb-hint: Used for all SMB/CIFS shares. Leave blank for anonymous access.
x-paths-smb-nfs-hint: NFS will be used for all mounts — no credentials needed.
x-paths-smb-configured-hint: Password already saved — leave blank to keep it.
x-paths-tooltip-libraries: Choose which media-server libraries Home Cinema Control should intercept. Libraries not selected will keep playing normally in their usual player.
x-paths-tooltip-all-libraries: Use this when every configured library is stored in a path the OPPO can mount. Disable it if only some libraries should be sent to the OPPO.
x-paths-tooltip-player-path: Enter the path as the OPPO sees it. The same text pattern can describe NFS or SMB/CIFS; the actual protocol is selected by the network authentication settings and the OPPO device list.
x-paths-tooltip-source-path: This is the path your media server indexes. Check the suggestion against your library settings before saving.
x-paths-tooltip-source-suggestion: Suggestions only transform text using a known verified mapping. They are not verified until you run Test path.
x-paths-tooltip-apply-suggestions: Applies the path pattern from a verified mapping to discovered media server folders. It can help with NFS or SMB/CIFS if the folder structure matches.
x-paths-tooltip-test-path: Asks the OPPO to mount the player-side path. It validates the mount path and protocol, not whether {server} can read the source path.
x-paths-tooltip-network-auth: Controls how the OPPO should access the network share. NFS usually needs no credentials; SMB/CIFS may need a username and password.
x-paths-tooltip-smb-toggle: Enable this when the OPPO accesses the share through SMB/CIFS. Leave it off when the OPPO should use NFS.
x-paths-tooltip-smb-pre-mount: For SMB/CIFS only. Works around an OPPO SMB compatibility quirk seen on some NAS devices, often related to SMBv1 being disabled on the NAS. Before mounting the real share, the OPPO briefly mounts another folder on the same NAS to prime its SMB session. Try this if SMB mounts fail and you cannot enable SMBv1 compatibility on the NAS.
x-setup-gate-title: Some features on this page won't work yet
x-setup-gate-discovery: Automatic discovery requires {server} to be connected.
x-setup-gate-testing: Path testing requires OPPO to be reachable.
x-setup-gate-manual: Set up paths manually (testing won't work)
x-sala-title: The room responds on its own
x-sala-subtitle: Decide when HCC should switch inputs, power devices, and return the room to normal.
x-sala-summary-title: TV and AV receiver control are optional and configured separately.
x-sala-help-step-tv: Enable TV control only if HCC should switch to the OPPO input and return to the media-server app.
x-sala-help-step-av: Enable the AV receiver if HCC should power it on or switch it to the OPPO input.
x-sala-help-step-save: Save each device after changing its fields. Successful tests are shown as tested for this session.
x-tv-subtitle: Automate input switching and power control during playback.
x-tv-card-subtitle: Input switching and return to the media-server app.
x-tv-mac-linux-only: Automatic MAC detection is only available on Linux. Enter the MAC address manually.
x-network-tooltip-scan: Detects active devices on your local network. Results appear as suggestions under the IP field so you don't have to type it manually.
x-tv-disabled-copy: HCC will not switch the TV input or try to return to the media-server app. This is valid if you do that manually or another device handles it.
x-tv-tooltip-section: Enable TV control if HCC should switch to the player input at startup and restore the media server app at the end.
x-tv-tooltip-model: LG uses network control. Scripts runs custom commands defined by you.
x-tv-tooltip-hdmi-input: Physical TV input where the OPPO is connected, or where the AV receiver receiving the OPPO is connected.
x-av-subtitle: Control your receiver's power and HDMI input as part of the playback sequence.
x-av-card-subtitle: Power control and OPPO HDMI input switching.
x-av-disabled-copy: HCC will not control the AV receiver. This is valid if you do not use a receiver, use a soundbar, or switch inputs manually.
x-av-tooltip-section: Enable the AV receiver only if HCC should power it on, switch its input, or power it off. If you use a soundbar, you usually do not need this.
x-av-tooltip-model: The model selects the control adapter. Scripts lets you define custom commands if your device is not supported.
x-av-tooltip-hdmi-delay: Pause after switching HDMI input. Useful if the receiver needs time to stabilize audio or video.
x-av-tooltip-always-on: Enable this if the receiver stays powered on. If disabled, HCC may power it off after playback.
x-av-tooltip-hdmi-input: Receiver input where the OPPO is connected. HCC switches to this input when playback starts.
x-diag-subtitle: Check playback, resources, version, and recent failures from one operational view.
x-diag-suggestion-TV_INPUT_SWITCH_FAILED: Check TV IP address, TV model, remote-control permissions and the configured player HDMI input.
x-diag-suggestion-AV_POWER_ON_FAILED: Check AV receiver IP address, model and network connectivity.
x-diag-suggestion-AV_INPUT_SWITCH_FAILED: Check the configured AV receiver input for the media player.
x-diag-reason-OPPO_MOUNT_FAILED-cifs: OPPO could not mount the media share via SMB/CIFS. {detail}
x-diag-reason-OPPO_MOUNT_FAILED-nfs: OPPO could not mount the media share via NFS. {detail}
x-diag-suggestion-OPPO_MOUNT_FAILED-cifs: Check SMB credentials, NAS SMB share permissions and that the SMB share is reachable from the OPPO.
x-diag-suggestion-OPPO_MOUNT_FAILED-nfs: Check NFS export settings, NAS NFS permissions and that the NFS share is reachable from the OPPO.
x-diag-suggestion-smb-hint: SMB was not attempted because it is not enabled in Media Paths settings — if this share is actually SMB/CIFS-only, enable SMB there and retry.
x-diag-reason-OPPO_STALE_MOUNT_SUSPECTED: OPPO network mount state may be stale. The path configuration may still be correct. Evidence: {detail}
x-diag-suggestion-OPPO_STALE_MOUNT_SUSPE: Do not change path mappings immediately. First retry after opening the OPPO network browser, stopping current playback, or rebooting the OPPO/NAS if the share appears stuck.
x-diag-suggestion-OPPO_PLAY_FAILED: Check that the media file exists, is readable and is supported by the player.
x-diag-suggestion-OPPO_PLAYBACK_TIMEOUT: Increase the playback start timeout only if the file normally takes long to open. Otherwise verify the media path and OPPO state.
x-diag-reason-OPPO_FINISH_IDLE_FAILED: OPPO did not reach/confirm idle during finish cleanup: {detail}
x-diag-suggestion-OPPO_FINISH_IDLE_FAILE: Check whether playback is still active on the player. If the UI is already idle, this may be a status-read failure rather than a media-path problem.
x-diag-suggestion-TV_APP_RESTORE_FAILED: Check TV network control and whether the previous app is still available. Playback has ended; this only affects room restoration.
x-diag-suggestion-AV_AUDIO_RESTORE_FAILE: Check AV receiver network control and the configured TV-audio input. Playback has ended; this only affects room restoration.
x-diag-reason-OPPO_ERROR_RECOVERY_FAILED: Playback error recovery could not stop or clean up the OPPO: {detail}
x-diag-suggestion-OPPO_ERROR_RECOVERY_FA: Stop playback manually on the OPPO and check whether the player remains responsive before starting another item.
x-diag-reason-TV_ERROR_RECOVERY_FAILED: Playback error recovery could not restore the TV app: {detail}
x-diag-suggestion-TV_ERROR_RECOVERY_FAIL: Return to the desired TV app manually and check TV network control settings.
x-diag-reason-AV_ERROR_RECOVERY_FAILED: Playback error recovery could not restore AV TV audio: {detail}
x-diag-suggestion-AV_ERROR_RECOVERY_FAIL: Restore the AV receiver input manually and check AV network control settings.
x-diag-reason-PATH_RESOLUTION_FAILED: Media path could not be resolved: {error}
x-diag-suggestion-PATH_RESOLUTION_FAILED: Check Media Paths configuration and ensure source paths match the media server's library paths.
x-diag-suggestion-MEDIA_SERVER_LIBRARY_P: Check the media server connection and token in Media Server settings.
x-diag-reason-PATH_INFERENCE_FAILED: Cannot infer a substitution rule from the anchor mapping.
x-diag-suggestion-PATH_INFERENCE_FAILED: Fill share paths manually, or choose an anchor mapping whose media server path and player-visible path share a common path component.
x-diag-suggestion-PATH_TEST_FAILED: Verify the media server path matches your library root and the player-visible network share path is reachable from the media player.
x-diag-reason-OPPO_PATH_TEST_UNAVAILABLE: Path test could not reach OPPO: {result}
x-diag-suggestion-OPPO_PATH_TEST_UNAVAIL: Power on the OPPO or verify its IP address before testing the media path.
x-diag-reason-OPPO_DEVICE_LIST_UNAVAILAB: OPPO did not provide a network device list during path test: {result}
x-diag-suggestion-OPPO_DEVICE_LIST_UNAVA: Open the OPPO network browser once, verify the NAS/share is visible, then retry the path test.
x-diag-reason-OPPO_UNAVAILABLE: OPPO player was not reachable when playback was requested.
x-diag-suggestion-OPPO_UNAVAILABLE: Ensure the OPPO is powered on or in standby and verify the IP address in Media Player settings.
x-diag-suggestion-MEDIA_SERVER_CONNECTIO: Check that the media server is running and reachable on your network, and that its address in Media Server settings is correct, then retry.
x-diag-suggestion-OPPO_CONNECTION_CHECK_: Check the device configuration and network connectivity, then retry the action.
x-diag-suggestion-TV_CONNECTION_TEST_FAI: Check the device configuration and network connectivity, then retry the action.
x-diag-suggestion-TV_SWITCH_INPUT_FAILED: Check the device configuration and network connectivity, then retry the action.
x-diag-suggestion-TV_RESTORE_APP_FAILED: Check the device configuration and network connectivity, then retry the action.
x-diag-suggestion-AV_POWER_OFF_FAILED: Check the device configuration and network connectivity, then retry the action.
x-diag-suggestion-AV_SWITCH_INPUT_FAILED: Check the device configuration and network connectivity, then retry the action.
x-status-tooltip-include-prerelease: Includes prereleases when checking for updates. It does not install anything automatically.
x-status-tooltip-webhook-url: Optional. If set, clicking Update will POST to this URL to trigger an automatic redeploy. Compatible with Watchtower, Coolify, and Portainer Business. Leave empty to get manual instructions instead.
x-status-update-triggered: Update triggered. Your container will restart with the latest image.
x-status-update-instructions-note: No webhook configured. Run this command on your server to update:
x-status-rollback-note: Previous version: {version}. To roll back, run on your server:
x-remote-subtitle: Play, pause, navigate, and inspect what is on without leaving HCC.
x-logs-subtitle: Filter and download HCC events when you need to understand exactly what happened.
x-logs-filter-empty: No log lines match this filter.
x-logs-levels-help: How much detail is recorded, both on this screen and in the container console.
x-tv-action-detect-inputs-tooltip: Finds the available HDMI inputs on the TV and saves the detected list.
x-tv-action-switch-player-tooltip: Switches the TV to the input where the OPPO is connected, or to the AV receiver that receives the OPPO.
x-tv-action-restore-media-server-tooltip: Reopens the {server} app on the TV.
x-tv-action-restore-media-server-not-con: Set up your media server first to reopen its app on the TV.
x-av-action-power-on-tooltip: Sends a power-on command to the AV receiver to verify that network control works.
x-av-action-power-off-tooltip: Sends a power-off command to the AV receiver to verify power control.
x-av-action-detect-inputs-tooltip: Finds the available AV receiver inputs and saves the detected list.
x-av-action-test-player-input-tooltip: Switches the AV receiver to the input where the OPPO is connected.
x-paths-tooltip-new-path: Adds a new mapping between the media server path and the OPPO-visible path.
x-paths-tooltip-delete-path: Remove this path mapping from the configuration.
x-paths-tooltip-edit-discovered-path: Create or adjust a mapping using this discovered path.
